key_themes: H1 FY2026 revenue decline (-16%), statutory loss, and £16.8m China (VadoTech) impairment due to tariffs/order delays
Margin resilience (18.6% adjusted operating margin held), rebounding order intake (£64m), strong cash, raised dividend, and reaffirmed FY guidance with 55-60% H2 weighting
Long-term ADAS/autonomous growth drivers intact but near-term execution risks from geopolitics, US order slowdown, and China weakness
sources_checked: Investors Chronicle, TipRanks, Yahoo Finance earnings transcripts
summary: AB Dynamics delivered a weak H1 FY2026 with 16% lower revenue, a statutory half-year loss driven by a major non-cash impairment on its Chinese testing business, and ongoing tariff-related disruptions slowing US and Chinese orders. Positives include resilient adjusted margins through cost control and mix, a recovering order book covering much of expected FY revenue, strong net cash, a 10% dividend increase, and reaffirmed full-year adjusted operating profit guidance. The biggest risk is delivering the heavily H2-weighted recovery amid persistent geopolitical and tariff uncertainty; analysts largely maintain bullish longer-term targets (avg ~2,116p vs current ~1,108p) but recent coverage reflects caution on near-term execution.[[1]](https://www.londonstockexchange.com/news-article/ABDP/half-year-report/17544726)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ABDP.L/earnings/ABDP.L-H1-2026-earnings_call-416337.html)[[3]](https://www.investorschronicle.co.uk/content/8b7d6590-9741-4d8b-b983-c7e78a1dc150)[[4]](https://www.tipranks.com/news/company-announcements/ab-dynamics-absorbs-china-impairment-but-holds-margins-and-backs-full-year-outlook)

recent_posts.highlight: Fund pitch highlighting quality business trading at cheap 11x EV/OPAT multiple with ~90% upside by end-2028, strong balance sheet, and predictable model.
Questions how confident investors should be in 'in line with expectations' full-year guidance after weak H1 adj. EPS -15% and H2 bias.
HOLD rating post-earnings; notes revenue decline but highlights catalysts like order recovery, H2 weighting, efficiencies, and acquisition opportunities.
Results described as 'uneventful' and in line with pre-release; notes good margin progress and reiterated guidance as positive, with shares rising ~4% intraday.
